title: Loom Draft Symmetry Forge
strategies: trivial: plain weave (tabby) -- the checker's internal baseline; fully symmetric but only two distinct motifs, F ~ lambda

greedy trap: fill threading/treadling with a varied pseudo-random assignment and repair illegal floats -- raises motif diversity but treats the drawdown as a free canvas and forgets symmetry, so random factors give mirror/rotation agreement ~0.5 and the whole score is halved
greedy alt: point-draw (diamond) twill -- perfectly symmetric but the monotone triangular wave revisits the same few shaft windows, so motif diversity is near zero

strong insight: the drawdown is the rank-<=S boolean product tieup[treadling][threading], so (1) make the threading/treadling FACTORS palindromic to get all mirror + rotation symmetry for free, (2) exploit that the float cap DECOUPLES per treadle (horizontal) and per shaft (vertical) into 1-D constraints on the factor sequences, (3) pack each half-sequence as a de-Bruijn-like code that maximizes distinct k-windows under those constraints
headroom above strong: trade a little symmetry for more diversity, or find a better distinct-window packing of the small-alphabet factor sequences
